high_priority_findings.finding: SOL_SHORT is the bot's #1 historical alpha (+$5807, 63.7% WR, 179 trades). Currently blocked: SOL_SHORT_edge_promote SUSPENDED_LIVE_CONTRADICTION + RULE_CONFLICT_SOL_SHORT at 100% gate. The live contradiction that triggered suspension is unknown — no record of what specific live data caused it.
high_priority_findings.action: PENDING_HUMAN_DECISION: (A) Allow SOL_SHORT probe at 50% gate size to re-test hypothesis. (B) Maintain full block. This is the highest-EV unresolved decision in the system.
high_priority_findings.expected_impact_if_unblocked: Historical: +$5807 on 179 trades (63.7% WR). If live contradiction holds: could lose ~$50-100 on 3-5 probe trades before re-blocking.

high_priority_findings.finding: CONF_60_70 data conflict: confidence_60_70_sweet_spot (93% confidence, SUSPENDED) claims conf 60-70% = +$2.19 avg profit. Active runtime rule conf_floor_70_v1 says 46.3% WR / -$2321 total — and penalizes -20pts. These rules cover the same confidence range but reached opposite conclusions.
high_priority_findings.action: POST-RESTART: Investigate dataset size difference. sweet_spot rule may be based on a specific symbol/regime subset while conf_floor_70_v1 aggregates all. Do NOT change conf_floor_70_v1 without investigation.
high_priority_findings.risk: If sweet_spot is correct, we're blocking profitable signals. If floor rule is correct, the suspension was appropriate.

high_priority_findings.impact_if_broken: Rules engine cannot self-calibrate. Bad rules never demoted. Good rules never promoted.

high_priority_findings.finding: RULE_CONFLICT_LONG_BIAS: SIDE_BIAS_LONG_filter (gate=80%) and SIDE_BIAS_LONG_SUSPENSION (gate=80%) at equal gates cancel each other. LONG trades are 74-82% of all trades but LONG WR = 29-32% vs SHORT WR much higher. Both rules agree on the problem but cannot both be active.
high_priority_findings.action: PENDING_HUMAN_DECISION: Keep SIDE_BIAS_LONG_filter (reduces LONG sizes) OR SIDE_BIAS_LONG_SUSPENSION (blocks LONG direction entirely at 80% gate). Cannot have both.
